title: Translation into Latin Europe
subtitle: Toledo, Paris, and the scholastic reception, 12th–14th centuries
directive: era showcase: Translation into Latin Europe

narrative.intro: Arabic alchemy crosses into Latin Christendom through Iberian translation centres. The pseudo-Geberian Summa Perfectionis becomes the canonical Latin theory; transmutation debates enter the universities.
narrative.provenance_note: Events from the ALCHEMYTIMELINEMAP corpus (largely draft-status entries); locations are event geocodes, not residences. Transmission arcs are reconstructed from the dated itineraries of named figures — absence of an arc reflects gaps in the record, not certainty of no contact.
narrative.design_note: Woodcut & Rubric: black letter and red rubric for the scholastic reception — quaestiones, condemnations, university Latin.
